summary: mnemonic-safety-check ships wallet/credential scanner with hardcoded C2
details: The package name advertises wallet seed-phrase safety checking, and the shipped code matches that cover story by performing the inverse: scanning the host for cryptocurrency wallets, credentials, and system information and exfiltrating to attacker-controlled infrastructure. wallet-scanner.js contains the wallet-targeting harvester (multiple POST sinks, curl invocations, ping reconnaissance, child_process execution combined with fs/https). scanner.js hardcodes a C2 endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (a personal GitHub Pages account, not a publisher domain) and combines os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), filesystem enumeration, and curl-based exfiltration. build-verifier.js adds a third exfiltration path with https.request POSTs and child_process exec, including an execSync('docker push...') line consistent with pushing harvested artifacts off-host. The combination of a wallet-scanning module, hostname/whoami/system enumeration, hardcoded non-publisher C2, and three independent exfiltration files in a package whose stated purpose is 'mnemonic safety' is an unambiguous credential/wallet stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
